description.en: Learn to package Python projects with pyproject.toml, build distributions, and publish to PyPI so anyone can pip install your library.
description.ta: pyproject.toml-உடன் Python projects-ஐ package செய்யவும், distributions build செய்யவும், PyPI-ல் publish செய்யவும் கற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Gift Wrapping

Vaathiyaar wraps a box with ribbon. "You've built a useful Python library. But right now it lives only on your laptop. To share it with the world, you need to **package** it — wrap it up nicely so anyone can `pip install` it."

### The Modern Way: pyproject.toml
- Replaced setup.py as the standard (PEP 621)
- Single file for project metadata, dependencies, and build config
- Used by pip, build, and all modern tools

### Project Structure
```
my-library/
├── pyproject.toml
├── README.md
├── LICENSE
├── src/
│   └── mylib/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       └── core.py
└── tests/
    └── test_core.py
```

### Build & Publish
```bash
pip install build twine
python -m build          # Creates dist/ with .tar.gz and .whl
twine upload dist/*      # Upload to PyPI
```

### Versioning
- Follow Semantic Versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
- 1.0.0 → 1.0.1 (bug fix)
- 1.0.0 → 1.1.0 (new feature, backward compatible)
- 1.0.0 → 2.0.0 (breaking change)

> **Key Concept:** Packaging transforms your project from 'code on my laptop' to 'installable library on PyPI'. Anyone in the world can then `pip install yourlib`.

explanation.en: Define which build backend to use
Build requires setuptools and wheel packages
Use setuptools as the build backend
Project metadata section — name, version, description
Package name on PyPI — must be unique globally
Short one-line description shown on PyPI
Runtime dependencies — pip installs these automatically
Extra dependency groups installed with pip install mylib[dev]
Configure entry points for CLI commands
Create CLI commands that users can run after install

instruction.en: Write a pyproject.toml for a library called 'mathutils' version 0.1.0, requiring Python >=3.10, with numpy as a dependency and pytest as a dev dependency.
instruction.ta: 'mathutils' என்ற library-க்கு version 0.1.0, Python >=3.10, numpy dependency, pytest dev dependency-உடன் pyproject.toml எழுதுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
# pyproject.toml
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68.0", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = # package name?
version = # version?
description = "Math utility functions"
requires-python = # minimum version?
dependencies = [
    # runtime deps?
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
    # dev deps?
]
practice_challenges.expected_output: Valid pyproject.toml with all fields filled

q.en: What file has replaced setup.py as the modern standard for Python packaging?
q.ta: Python packaging-க்கான நவீன standard-ஆக setup.py-ஐ எந்த file மாற்றியது?

q.en: What tool uploads built packages to PyPI?
q.ta: Build செய்யப்பட்ட packages-ஐ PyPI-க்கு upload செய்ய எந்த tool பயன்படுகிறது?

q.en: In semantic versioning 2.3.1, what does the '3' represent?
